message:
feat(io): IndexedDB async storage backend and MessagePort/BroadcastChannel streaming

browser_storage: adds AsyncStorageHostBackend trait with async set/get/
delete/list_keys/clear_namespace methods for browser-native storage APIs
like IndexedDB whose host interfaces are inherently asynchronous. The
trait uses StorageHostFuture type aliases that are Send on native (for
test executor compatibility) and non-Send on wasm32. BrowserStorageAdapter
gains register_async_host_backend and an async_host_backends registry.

The wasm32 implementation includes full IndexedDB integration using
web-sys IdbFactory/IdbDatabase/IdbObjectStore/IdbTransaction/IdbRequest
types, with proper error handling through IdbRequest events and JsFuture
bridging.

browser_stream: adds BrowserMessagePort, BrowserMessageChannel,
BrowserMessageChannelPair, BrowserBroadcastChannel types that provide
structured async read/write over Web API MessagePort and
BroadcastChannel interfaces. BrowserMessagePayload supports both string
and binary (ArrayBuffer) messages. BrowserMessageState tracks port
lifecycle. BrowserMessageError provides typed error variants.

The wasm32 path wires up real web-sys MessageChannel/MessagePort/
BroadcastChannel/MessageEvent bindings with Closure-based event
handlers. The native path provides test-friendly in-memory
implementations using Arc<Mutex<VecDeque>> channels.

Byte budget arithmetic in BrowserReadableStream and BrowserWritableStream
corrected to use u64 consistently for remaining-budget calculations,
preventing truncation on 32-bit targets when total_read/total_written
approaches u32::MAX.

io/mod.rs: re-exports new browser stream types.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
